title: Large Language Models as Intelligent Tutoring Systems: A Systematic Review
authors: Kasneci E., Sessler K., Küchemann S., Bannert M., Dementieva D., Fischer F., Gasser U., Groh G., Günnemann S., Hüllermeier E.
study_design: Systematic review of 87 studies on LLM-based ITS across grade levels. RCT evidence shows avg +0.4 SD learning gain vs. control.

title: Equity Gaps in AI-Assisted Learning: Evidence from 42 U.S. School Districts
authors: Escueta M., Nickow A.J., Oreopoulos P., Quan V.
journal: Journal of Policy Analysis and Management
study_design: Quasi-experimental design across 42 districts (N=210 000 students). Black and Hispanic students show 60% lower AI tool adoption.

title: Does AI Feedback Improve Writing Quality? A Randomized Controlled Trial
authors: Tate T., Doroudi S., Ritchie D., Xu Y., Warschauer M.
study_design: RCT with N=1 840 secondary students. AI feedback group improved writing rubric scores by 18% vs. control (p<0.001).

title: Teacher Readiness for AI Integration: A National Survey of 5 000 US Educators
authors: Gado I., Ferguson R., Roofe C.
study_design: Cross-sectional survey of 5 012 K-12 teachers. Only 23% report confidence using AI tools; PD access is the strongest predictor.

title: Measuring AI Literacy in Secondary Students: Scale Development and Validation
authors: Long D., Magerko B., Laboon K., Buss J.
study_design: Psychometric validation across 3 cohorts (N=2 210). 5-factor AI literacy scale; Cronbach α=0.91; strong predictive validity.

title: The Homework Help Divide: Low-Income Students and Generative AI Access
authors: Reich J., Ito M., Watkins S.C.
study_design: Mixed methods across 8 urban districts. Free-tier AI tools used by 31% of low-income students vs. 74% in high-income schools.

title: Agentic AI in the Classroom: Early Evidence from Pilot Programs
authors: Dede C., Grotzer T., Kapur M., Klopfer E.
study_design: Pre-post pilot across 12 classrooms. Agent-assisted inquiry improves science reasoning scores (+0.3 SD) but effect varies by teacher.

title: Generative AI and Academic Integrity: A Longitudinal Study in Higher Education
authors: Cotton D., Cotton P., Shipway J.R.
study_design: Longitudinal (3 semesters
score_rigor: N=4 800). AI-assisted work detected in 38% of assignments; honor code clarity reduces misconduct by 52%.

title: Workforce Displacement and AI Adoption: Evidence from 50 US Labor Markets
authors: Acemoglu D., Johnson S., Loebbing B.
study_design: Difference-in-differences using BLS data. Each 10% rise in AI adoption correlates with 3.2% wage decline for non-college workers.

title: AI Tutors for English Language Learners: Closing the Reading Gap
authors: Gandara P., Hopkins M., Martinez-Wenzl M.
study_design: RCT with ELL students grades 3-5 (N=980). AI tutor group closed 62% of reading gap vs. grade-level peers after one semester.

title: ROI of AI Adoption in K-12: A Cost-Effectiveness Meta-Analysis
authors: Kraft M.A., Blazar D., Hogan D.
study_design: Meta-analysis of 34 RCTs. Average cost per SD gain: AI tools ($420) vs. tutoring ($3 200) vs. class-size reduction ($9 800).

title: Responsible AI in Schools: A Framework for Equity-Centered Deployment
authors: Williamson B., Eynon R., Potter J.
study_design: Qualitative synthesis of 22 district AI policies. Equity-centered frameworks include 6 core dimensions; adoption lags by 18 months avg.

title: Scaling Personalized Learning with LLMs: Evidence from 200 Schools
authors: Koedinger K.R., Booth J.L., Klahr D., Carvalho P.F.
study_design: Cluster-RCT across 200 schools (N=95 000). LLM-personalized math curriculum improves proficiency rates by 11pp over 1 school year.

title: Detecting Bias in AI Grading Systems: A Systematic Audit
authors: Mayfield E., Black A.W., Madaio M.
study_design: Audit of 8 widely used AI grading platforms. Racial and socioeconomic bias detected in 6 of 8 systems; open-source models worst.

title: The AI Skills Gap: What Employers Want and What Schools Produce
authors: Burning Glass Institute
journal: Burning Glass Institute / Jobs for the Future
study_design: Analysis of 4.2M job postings + 300 employer interviews. AI skill demand grew 340% in 2 years; schools produce 1 graduate per 12 openings.

title: ChatGPT in the Classroom: One Year On — A Longitudinal Assessment
authors: Mollick E., Mollick L.
study_design: Longitudinal study (N=3 400
score_rigor: 4 semesters). Consistent +0.5 SD improvement in critical thinking for structured AI use vs. control.

title: Community-Based AI Literacy Programs: Evidence from 15 Cities
authors: Lee V.R., Pinkard N., Gomez L.
study_design: Pre-post design across 15 community programs (N=4 100 adult learners). 8-week AI literacy curriculum improves workforce readiness scores 34%.

title: Does Explainable AI Help Teachers? A Mixed-Methods Study
authors: Holstein K., McLaren B.M., Aleven V.
journal: International Journal of AI in Education
study_design: Mixed methods (N=120 teachers). XAI dashboards improve teacher intervention accuracy by 28%; requires 4h+ onboarding to be effective.

title: AI and Special Education: Outcomes for Students with Learning Disabilities
authors: Fuchs L.S., Fuchs D., Malone A.S.
study_design: RCT with N=740 students with IEPs. AI-assisted reading intervention closes 71% of gap vs. general ed peers; effect size d=0.82.

title: Generative AI Policy Adoption Across 50 State Education Departments
authors: Future of Privacy Forum
study_design: Document analysis of all 50 state AI education policies. 28 states have formal guidance; only 12 address student data privacy explicitly.

title: The Price of Intelligence: AI Compute Costs and Accessibility
authors: Sevilla J., Heim L., Ho A., Besiroglu T., Hobbhahn M., Villalobos P.
study_design: Longitudinal cost tracking across 120 frontier models (2018-2025). Inference cost falls 40% per year; frontier model access still 10x cost of open models.

title: Indigenous AI Literacy: Centering Community Knowledge in Technology Education
authors: Tachine A.R., Bird Bear C., Minthorn B.
study_design: Participatory action research across 7 tribal colleges. Community-co-designed AI curriculum improves relevance scores by 2.1x vs. standard modules.

title: From Pilot to Scale: What Makes AI EdTech Programs Succeed?
authors: Education Trust; Results for America
study_design: Case study analysis of 18 district AI EdTech scale-ups. Top factors: principal buy-in

title: Agentic AI Tutors: Longitudinal Outcomes Across 3 School Years
authors: Schmucker R., Mitchell J., Koedinger K.
study_design: 3-year longitudinal quasi-experiment (N=8 400). Agentic tutors sustain +0.38 SD math gains year-over-year; strongest for Title I schools.

title: AI Benchmark Inflation: Why Leaderboard Rankings May Mislead Practitioners
authors: Biderman S., Ustun B., Gao L., Bisk Y.
study_design: Empirical analysis of 200+ benchmarks. 68% show saturation or contamination; practitioner-task correlation drops to r=0.31 for top-tier models.

title: Return on Investment of AI Adoption in Higher Education: A Multi-Institution Study
authors: Deloitte Center for Higher Education
study_design: Financial analysis across 45 universities. Avg 3-year ROI of AI administrative tools: 240%; instructional AI tools: 180%; risk of equity regression without guardrails.

title: AI and the Teaching Profession: Threat or Transformation?
authors: Papay J.P., Kraft M.A., Reimers F.
study_design: Survey + administrative data (N=12 000 teachers
score_rigor: 8 states). AI adoption correlates with role expansion not reduction; union contracts key moderator.

title: Equity-Aware AI: Designing for Marginalized Learners from the Ground Up
authors: Barron B., Darling-Hammond L., Pea R., Bransford J.
study_design: Systematic review of 58 studies. Co-design with marginalized communities increases engagement by 2.8x and reduces dropout in AI-assisted programs by 41%.

title: The State of AI in K-12 Education 2026: Annual Benchmark Report
authors: RAND Corporation
study_design: National probability survey (N=6 800 school leaders) + administrative data. AI tool adoption reached 61% of US districts; equity gap widened 18pp since 2023.

title: Generative AI for Formative Assessment: A Large-Scale RCT
authors: Pane J.F., Griffin B.A., McCaffrey D.F., Karam R.
study_design: RCT across 310 secondary schools (N=48 000). GenAI formative feedback improves end-of-year test scores by 0.29 SD; largest effect for ELL and IEP students.
